About

Professional Bio & Value Proposition

Who I am, where I'm headed, and the value I bring to a data-driven team.

Professional Bio

I am a data analyst with experience in collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to support business decision-making. I am passionate about transforming complex datasets into meaningful insights and leveraging emerging technologies to solve real-world problems.

My professional interests include data analytics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning. I continuously seek opportunities to expand my technical and leadership skills while helping organizations make informed, data-driven decisions.

As I continue my professional journey, my goal is to combine analytical thinking, technology, and innovation to create solutions that generate measurable business impact.

My Value Proposition

I bring a unique combination of analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and a commitment to continuous learning. My ability to transform raw data into actionable insights and communicate findings effectively allows me to support strategic decision-making and organizational growth.

I am adaptable, curious, and passionate about using data and technology to solve complex problems and create meaningful value.

Artifact 01

Professional Leadership and Self-Assessment Reflection

Complete
Introduction

This artifact represents my completion of leadership and change management self-assessments focused on AI/ML integration and professional growth.

Description

The assessments helped me identify strengths in communication, adaptability, and problem-solving while highlighting opportunities to strengthen technical leadership and project management skills.

Objective

To evaluate my leadership capabilities and create a professional development plan.

Process
  1. Completed leadership self-assessments.
  2. Evaluated strengths and weaknesses.
  3. Connected findings to career goals.
  4. Developed an improvement strategy.
Tools and Technologies Used
  • Self-assessment frameworks
  • Reflection techniques
  • Microsoft Word
  • Research articles
Value Proposition

This artifact demonstrates self-awareness, leadership development, and commitment to continuous learning.

Unique Value: It showcases both technical and interpersonal competencies that are essential in modern data and technology environments.

Relevance: Organizations need professionals who can adapt to change, lead teams, and continuously develop their skills. This artifact demonstrates my readiness to grow into those responsibilities.

References

Center for Creative Leadership. (2024). How to be a Successful Change Leader.
Change Strategists. (2023). How to Assess Change Management Skills.

Artifact 02

AI-Assisted Resume Optimization for Technical Recruiting

Complete
Title

AI-Assisted Resume Optimization for Technical Recruiting: Applying Judgment-Driven AI Collaboration to Real-World Deliverables.

Objective

To use a generative AI assistant as a working partner — not a black box — for tailoring technical consultant resumes to specific job descriptions, while preserving factual accuracy and professional integrity, and to practice directing AI output toward a business goal while critically evaluating that output.

Process
  1. Supplied the AI with a candidate resume and a target job description.
  2. Had the AI assess genuine skill overlap before writing anything.
  3. Where overlap was minimal, the AI declined to fabricate experience and proposed honest alternatives.
  4. Where overlap was partial, added tailored content using supporting/contributing language and flagged the riskiest claims.
  5. Validated and visually reviewed each edited document before delivery.
Tools and Technologies Used
  • Claude (Anthropic) as AI collaborator
  • Python for XML parsing and validation
  • Microsoft Word (.docx/OOXML) structure
  • LibreOffice for PDF rendering and visual QA
Value Proposition

This artifact demonstrates applied AI/ML competency that goes beyond prompting a model to "write something": it shows directing an AI tool toward a concrete outcome, critically evaluating its output, and using the AI's own pushback as a signal to adjust strategy.

Unique Value: Shows oversight and judgment in AI collaboration, distinct from Artifact 1's focus on self-assessment.

Relevance: Responsible AI/ML integration in the workplace requires exactly this kind of critical human oversight.

Reflection

Customization for Audience: Reframed practical recruiting work into a case study in AI oversight for an instructor audience, abstracting candidate specifics into general categories (mismatch, partial overlap, strong match).

Lessons Learned: A good AI collaborator should sometimes say no, and that pushback is useful information rather than an obstacle to route around.

Feedback Received: The AI's own reasoning about risky vs. defensible claims functioned like a peer reviewer flagging risk before the deliverable went out.

Revisions Applied: Abandoned the mismatch case rather than fabricating content, minimized risky claims in the partial-overlap case, and checked chronological plausibility in the strong-match case.

📄 Download Artifact 2 (Word Document)
Artifact 03

Prompt-Engineering Quick-Reference Guide

Complete
Title

Prompt-Engineering Quick-Reference Guide for AI-Assisted Resume Tailoring.

Introduction

A standalone, reusable quick-reference guide that distills practical lessons from real AI-assisted resume-tailoring work (see Artifact 2) into prompt patterns and do's/don'ts another person could apply immediately.

Description

Includes a do/don't checklist for keeping AI-assisted resume tailoring honest and defensible, plus four ready-to-use prompt patterns walking through assessing fit, handling mismatches, tailoring genuine overlap, and validating results.

Objective

Convert tacit judgment calls from hands-on AI collaboration into an explicit, teachable reference that helps others avoid fabricated experience and chronologically implausible claims.

Process
  1. Reviewed real prompting decisions and AI pushback moments from Artifact 2.
  2. Separated what worked from what caused problems.
  3. Converted patterns into a do/don't table and four prompt templates.
  4. Structured the guide to stand alone without needing the original project's context.
Tools and Technologies Used
  • Claude (Anthropic) for drafting guide language
  • Microsoft Word for the deliverable format
  • Real prompting transcripts from Artifact 2 as source material
Value Proposition

Shows the ability to convert hands-on experience into a reusable resource others can act on — distinct from self-assessment (Artifact 1) or direct AI oversight (Artifact 2).

Unique Value: Demonstrates documenting and teaching responsible AI use to others, not just using a tool personally.

Relevance: Converting personal experience into team-level guidance is core to responsible AI adoption in any AI/ML leadership role.

References

Portfolio Artifact 2: AI-Assisted Resume Optimization for Technical Recruiting (this course, same author) — source material for the patterns documented in this guide.
